home_jurisdiction_name.description: Surviving entity's name as filed in its home (non-Maine) jurisdiction. Distinct from merger.surviving_entity.new_name (post-merger name change). Compare with MBCA-10/MNPCA-10 which use a combined merger.surviving_entity.name_and_jurisdiction recital — this form splits name and jurisdiction into separate widgets.

home_jurisdiction.description: State or country where the surviving entity is organized. Parallel to entity.home_jurisdiction (foreign-qualification convention) but scoped under merger.surviving_entity for clarity in the merger namespace.

maine_authorization_date.description: Date the surviving foreign entity was authorized to transact business in Maine, if it was previously qualified. Blank means it was never qualified in Maine.

new_name.description: New home-jurisdiction name of the surviving entity if the merger changes its name. Blank means unchanged.

home_jurisdiction_name.description: Nonsurviving (merged-away) entity's name in its home jurisdiction. Form provides one inline slot; additional nonsurviving entities are recorded via attached pages (no widget for them).

home_jurisdiction.description: State or country where the nonsurviving entity is organized.

maine_authorization_date.description: Date the nonsurviving entity was authorized to transact business in Maine, if it was previously qualified. Blank means it was never qualified in Maine.

certified_copy_attached.description: Boolean: whether a certified copy of the merger (or certificate of merger) from the home jurisdiction is attached. The page-0 instruction line 'Must attach a Certified Copy of the Merger or a Certificate of Merger' makes this attachment mandatory for the filing to be accepted. No widget binds this fact — it is a manual/process check.

entities.description: Entity name(s) the filing covers, as listed on the cover block.
